title: Make a Simple Home Inventory
summary: List your important belongings for insurance claims, moving, or personal records.
learnerPromise: You will be able to create a basic list of your valuable items with descriptions and photos that can support an insurance claim or help you stay organized during a move.
whyItMatters: If items are damaged, stolen, or lost, an inventory makes it much easier to file an insurance claim or remember what you owned. It also helps you estimate what you need when moving to a new place.
realLifeUses: List electronics and appliances for a renter's insurance claim after a break-in
Photo-document valuables before a move so you can check if anything arrives damaged
Keep a record of serial numbers for phones, laptops, and bikes in case of theft
Estimate the value of your belongings when shopping for insurance coverage
result: A dated list of your most important items with descriptions, approximate values, and photos.
youWillLearn: Decide which items are worth listing (high value or hard to replace)
Write a short, useful description of each item including brand and model if known
Take a clear photo that shows the item and any identifying marks or serial numbers
Store the inventory where it will not be lost along with the items it describes
miniChallenge: Pick the five most valuable items in your room and list them with a one-line description and a photo each.
steps: Choose one room and identify the five to ten most valuable or important items in it
For each item, write the name, brand or model if known, and approximate value
Take a clear photo of each item and a close-up of any serial number or label
Save the list and photos together using a cloud app, email, or external storage
Set a reminder to update the list when you acquire or remove significant items
completionCriteria: You have a dated list with at least five items that includes descriptions and photos
The list is stored somewhere separate from the items themselves (cloud, email, etc.)
commonProblems: It feels overwhelming to inventory everything, so people never start
Photos are blurry or do not show identifying details
The list gets lost because it is stored on the same device that could be stolen
tips: Start with one room or category—electronics are usually the most valuable
Serial numbers matter more than exact photos for insurance purposes
Email the list to yourself or use a cloud service so it is accessible from anywhere
Receipts and warranties are useful—photo them and add them to your inventory file
